_comment: Claude Apex V7 — 9 domains, 40 JIT rules. Load only the rules relevant to the current prompt.

rules: Keep responses focused — avoid unnecessary exploration
Prefer targeted edits over full file rewrites
Skip verbose explanations — action over narration
Consider suggesting /compact or spawning a subagent for remaining work

rules.text: NEVER use relative paths in code — use absolute. NEVER use absolute paths when referencing files to user — use relative.
NEVER run independent tool calls sequentially — batch them in parallel.
NEVER mark tasks complete without validation — test everything, require proof.
Always use TypeScript strict mode, never plain JavaScript.
Use Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes, no custom CSS unless necessary.
Keep components under 300 lines, split into modules.
Run npm run build after code changes to verify.
Use context7 MCP for library documentation lookup before writing framework code.
Test on mobile viewport first. Mobile-first responsive design is the default.
Use semantic HTML for accessibility. Include ARIA labels where needed.

Before coding UI components from scratch, check if 21st.dev Magic MCP (@21st-dev/magic) has a ready-made component. Use premium-web-design skill patterns for animations. Generate base with 21st.dev, customize with hand-coded GSAP/Framer Motion patterns.

For design reference, check awesome-design-md (Airbnb/Apple/Linear/Spotify/Stripe DESIGN.md files at ~/.claude/references/awesome-design-md/) AND premium-web-design references (analyzed sites at ~/.claude/skills/premium-web-design/references/).
Generate non-Latin scripts (Arabic, Hebrew, CJK, etc.) inside Python scripts only, never in terminal commands — terminals corrupt encoding.
Use arabic-reshaper + python-bidi for PDF. Use raw Unicode + rtl=1 XML for PPTX. NEVER use arabic-reshaper for PPTX (breaks letter joining).
All presentations must be phone-readable: 18pt+ body text, 28pt+ headers.
On Linux/WSL, pip install always uses --break-system-packages flag. On Windows/macOS this is not needed.
NEVER mix RTL and LTR scripts in the same text run. Always separate into different paragraphs or text boxes.
Use consistent brand colors on every slide. Define them once in a config object and reference everywhere. Document your brand palette in the project's CLAUDE.md.
Export Word via python-docx, PDF via reportlab or pypdf, PPTX via python-pptx, XLSX via openpyxl. Do not shell out to MS Office unless running on Windows with COM automation (comtypes).
Use exa-web-search MCP for semantic search.
Use firecrawl MCP for website scraping.
Cross-reference minimum 3 sources before stating facts.
Save research output with a date prefix (YYYY-MM-DD) in a dedicated research folder for version tracking.
When research must be delivered in multiple languages, generate each language version separately — never translate in a terminal stream.
git add specific files only. NEVER git add -A or git add .
git commit with descriptive message following conventional commits format.
git push origin main (or your default branch) before deploying.
vercel --prod and wait for completion. Note the deployment URL.
If you use a canonical alias, ALWAYS run 'vercel alias set <new-deployment-url> <your-canonical-domain>' after deploy. NEVER skip this — clients/users hit the canonical URL, not the per-deploy URL.
Verify deployment with Playwright screenshot after alias is set.
Reference the jurisdiction's primary commercial/labor law first. Specialize the rule loadout for your region (Qatar, UAE, US, EU, UK, etc.) — edit the recall keywords to match.
All legal output in the jurisdiction's formal legal register (Modern Standard Arabic for GCC, plain English for US/UK, etc.).
Never provide definitive legal advice. Frame every output as analysis and recommend engaging a licensed attorney for authoritative counsel.
Cross-reference with regional regulations when applicable (GCC, EU, state-level, federal, etc.).
Cite sources for every legal claim — statute number, case citation, or regulation reference.
For unauthenticated web content, prefer WebSearch, WebFetch, exa-web-search MCP, or context7 MCP. Use browser MCPs (Playwright) only when you need rendering, JavaScript execution, or visual capture.

Tasks requiring a logged-in browser session (authenticated Instagram, LinkedIn, private dashboards) should be delegated to a session-cookie-based scraper like instaloader, or to the user's browser extension. Never attempt interactive login in Claude Code's Bash tool — it cannot handle password prompts.
ALWAYS query claude-mem (mem-search skill) for prior context BEFORE reading memory files or project history.
ALWAYS check if a graphify knowledge graph exists for the current project BEFORE reading raw files. Graph path: graphify-out/graph.json in the project root.
Graph query cost ~1000 tokens; raw file read can be 10,000+. Prefer the graph unless the task specifically requires file content (edit, debug, review).
